title: Failure on pushing manifest with multi-arch images to quay
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

BUG REPORT INFORMATION

**Description**
I tried to build multi-arch images using build `without containerfile` strategy and added them to manifest. When I tried to push the manifest it fails with the [error.](https://github.com/divyansh42/buildah-build/runs/4043495184?check_suite_focus=true#step:20:58)
However, when I did this with containerfile everything worked perfectly fine.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build multi-arch image
2. Add them to the manifest
3. Push the manifest to `quay.io`

**Describe the results you received:**
This is the [GitHub Actions job](https://github.com/divyansh42/buildah-build/actions/runs/1397832299/workflow#L134) that builds the image adds it to the manifest and pushes the manifest to quay.io

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should be able to push the manifest.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/now 100:1.21.3-1 amd64 [installed,local]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```

Do you have a simple reproducer?
@nalind @vrothberg PTAL

Yes, this [GitHub workflow](https://github.com/divyansh42/buildah-build/blob/test/.github/workflows/multiarch.yml) can be run to reproduce the issue, the only configuration that it requires is setting up the image registry credentials.
You can set `REGISTRY_PASSWORD` as the GitHub workflow secret and change `quay.io/diagrawa` to `your_registry/namespace`
Any chance you could break it into a simpler reproducer with a number of buildah commands?  I am unlikely to find the time to analyze a ~300 lines workflow file.
Yes, I could break it for you.

https://github.com/divyansh42/buildah-build/blob/test/.github/workflows/test_multiarch.yml this workflow just builds the jar of the spring-petclinic project. Then build amd64 and arm64v8 images, add both the images to the manifest and finally push the manifest.

This is the [workflow run](https://github.com/divyansh42/buildah-build/runs/4046901936?check_suite_focus=true) of the above workflow file.

**These are the commands:**
```
buildah from docker.io/arm64v8/alpine:3.14
buildah config --entrypoint ./entrypoint.sh --port 8080 --arch arm64v8 --workingdir . alpine-working-container
buildah commit --format docker --squash alpine-working-container test-multiarch:arm64v8

buildah from docker.io/amd64/alpine:3.14
buildah config --entrypoint ./entrypoint.sh --port 8080 --arch amd64 --workingdir . alpine-working-container-1
buildah commit --format docker --squash alpine-working-container-1 test-multiarch:amd64

buildah manifest create test-multiarch
buildah manifest add test-multiarch test-multiarch:arm64v8
buildah manifest add test-multiarch test-multiarch:amd64

buildah manifest push test-multiarch:latest docker://quay.io/diagrawa/test-multiarch:latest --all
```

This push fails on `quay.io`. However, when I tried to push to `docker.io`, it got successfully pushed.
Please let me know if you need any other details.

Sounds like quay.io does not like the manifest format.
@mtrmac @nalind @giuseppe PTAL
When I built those images using containerfile, I was able to push them to quay.io as well as docker.io
Update: Tried this with the `oci` format instead of `docker` and it worked.

So quay.io does not like the way we create a manifest list if the inside images are docker images.

@mtrmac @vrothberg @nalind are we embedding OCI identifiers into the manifest list that is confusing quay.io?

> So quay.io does not like the way we create a manifest list if the inside images are docker images.

I don't think this is the case. As, when I created manifest with the docker images built from containerfile, I was able to push that manifest. This problem is happening when I built the images without containerfile.
If you examine the manifest for both cases, do you notice any differences?

I can’t see anything _obvious_ about how this fails, and it’s frustrating that Quay.io combines strict validation with seemingly unhelpful errors. (Hopefully we are not throwing away errors the registry is actually reporting?)

Comparing the `--debug` output of the failing/succeeding `buildah manifest push` commands might be helpful.

---

Per https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/image-index.md , `architecture: arm64v8` is not valid. Does this work with valid values?

> Does this work with valid values?

Tried changing it to valid architecture, but no luck there.

Also, I couldn't find any obvious thing in the logs with the `--debug` output of the failing/succeeding command. I am pasting those logs here.

**Failed command logs**
```
